causal_question: For cities that switched to single-member district elections in 2016, how did the switch affect the natural log of multifamily units permitted between 2015 and 2017 compared to otherwise-similar cities that had not switched by 2017, adjusting for population, homeownership and vacancy rates, median home value and income, and prior minority electoral success?
vague_question: What happened to the amount of new multifamily housing that got permitted in cities that switched to district-based city council elections in 2016 when you look at the years just before and after the change?
